entry.title: SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud (C4C) 2605 Pre-Release Briefing
entry.content:
Hi All,

SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud (C4C) Pre-Release Briefing presentation for the upcoming 2605 (May 2026) release is now available. We have some key innovations to be shared as part of SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud 2605 Release.

Platform & Integration:

Mandatory Adoption – Convert Embedded SAC Stories to Optimized Design Experience (ODE) Format

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) has introduced Optimized Design Experience, which enables content within an Embedded SAC story to load quickly. Existing stories that are in ‘classic’ mode will remain in the classic format until they are converted to ODE format. Please follow the simple steps mentioned in our help documentation here to convert your existing stories into the ODE format. The help documentation also contains links from SAC documentation on this topic.

Please note that SAC plans to deprecate the classic format by the end of Q2 2026. Hence, customers are advised to perform the conversion of their classic Embedded SAC stories before the mentioned timeframe.

Transition Tools:

  • Readiness Check Tool (RCT) | Shows installed Add-Ons in V1 tenant - The “Installed Add-Ons” tab shows Add-Ons applied in the V1 tenant that the RCT is running on.
  • Data Transfer Tool (DTT) | Introducing ”Count Summary” in Org Structures - Introducing “Count Summary” with “Org Structures” to classify transfer data instances within business object.
  • Data Transfer Tool (DTT) | Key User Extensibility field Transfer in BP Relationships - Business Partner (BP) Relationships support Transfer of Key User Extensibility Field transfer in DTT.
  • Data Transfer Tool (DTT) | Transfer Code List Mapping as part of BC Transfer - “Transfer All BC” now includes Code List Mapping.

Feeds | Integration with SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition - As an Administrator, you have an option to use SAP Jam (until 31st December 2026 due to deprecation of SAP Jam collaboration) or SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition for collaboration via Feeds. This was communicated earlier in the SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud 2511 Release Briefing. Kindly plan to move from SAP Jam and start using SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition for collaboration via Feeds based on your requirements.

SAP Sales Cloud DVP Add-On:

  • Dynamic Visit Planning Add-On | Outstation Planning - Dynamic visit planning now allows to curate plans with stays away from office/home to manage territories.
  • Dynamic Visit Planning Add-On | Plan my day on filtered list - Plan my day would only focus on accounts with highest score, Now it respects the filters as well.

SAP Service Cloud:

  • Support in C4C Ticket for FSM Data Retention in C4C-FSM Integration - When a service call object is marked for soft delete or hard delete by the data retention policy in FSM by an Administrator, the corresponding replicated ticket still remains in C4C. Any update on C4C ticket will not trigger an outbound sync to FSM anymore.
  • Enabled Read/Write access (PSM release) for Maintenance Plan field MaintenancePlanNextRunMDROID

SAP Agent Desktop Add-On:

  • Agent Desktop Add-On | New Ticket Form on Incoming Call Acceptance - The 'New Ticket Create' form would display alongside the Agent Desktop session when the agent accepts an incoming phone call.

Thanks,

SAP Sales Cloud & SAP Service Cloud Product Management Team

entry.title: I am getting a warning message while opening a work order "Assembly ###### is flagged for deletion"
entry.content:
I am getting a warning message while opening a work order "Assembly ###### is flagged for deletion"
This ###### is a material and is mainatined as component in teh same order and deletion flag is set at cleint level for that material.

so even for other order and its components have set to deletion flag at client level, but they dont show a similar message ()"Assembly ###### is flagged for deletion") while opening the work order.

entry.title: Need to turn off specific Ariba Supplier Notifications going out in TEST
entry.content:
For background I work on SAP ECC, so I'm a novice with Ariba.

We've been live with Ariba for a while now, but are still on-boarding suppliers a handful at a time. When we work with suppliers to on-board them, we first on-board them in the TEST Ariba environment and once everything passes, then they are on-boarded to Production Ariba. While onboarding in TEST they want the notifications to be sent to the Supplier, etc. But after they are on-boarded to Production Ariba, they want to turn off the notifications in TEST and only send the supplier notifications in Production. But they still want to send notifications out of TEST for other suppliers that are still testing the onboard process.

So basically, turn off notifications in TEST for most suppliers and only have the notifications in TEST for the small set of suppliers currently being onboarded in TEST.

We had a situation where the MPR jobs were run wide open in our QA SAP system and it pushed a bunch of updates to TEST Ariba that also sent notifications to a bunch of suppliers in TEST. The suppliers thought the notifications were production notifications (even though the notifications said TEST) so they logged a bunch of issues with us thinking they were getting duplicate data in production. Our business is asking that we NEVER run MRP in QA wide open, but there really isn't a good way to prevent this and there are times where we may need to run it wide open in our QA environment for valid testing reasons. I don't think this is a valid long-term solution.

Is there a way for us to control the notifications we send out of TEST Ariba? Or does each supplier have to go in and turn off the TEST notifications themselves?

Or is there a better way to handle this that I'm not currently thinking of?

entry.title: Re: How to calculate the current salary data for Proratio...
entry.content:
Hi @wanlapha_17-09 

Can you open individual worksheets on the Current Salary column and check whether it is also not prorated there (which is my guess). 

If my guess is correct then you need to add the proration configuration, information can be found here: https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/fte-and-proration-examples-in-compensation/ba-p/13503901

as well as in the implementation workbook.

 

FYI Budget Assignment feature is use-as-is, meaning we can only turn it on or off but we can't configure its screen or setting. It is not being prioritized for enhancements by product either (just like Hierarchy Based Approvals) as far as I know. For budget configuration leading practices, see links of first paragraph here: https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/budget-design-process-for-salary-increases-a-functional-overview/ba-p/14103887

 

Thanks

Xavier

entry.title: Value accelerators Library - which use for new S4 Hana Private Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

I want to user SAP Signavio Process Explorer to import SAP Best Practices Library for a new S4 Hana Private Cloud project implementation.

S4 hana release is 2025 FPS1.

In the Value Accelerator Library  I have two options:

1) Process Landscape Design & Fit-to-Standard in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (it contain Best Practices but no specific version is indicated)

2) Process design and standardization for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (it contains Best Practices of S4 2025 FPS0)

Based on Business Process Diagram number (883 the same) and Dictionary Entry number (about 17K for both) I don't know which I shoud use for my greenfield S4 hana project.

Any suggestion?

Regards

 Maurizio

entry.title: Re: Does PRIMARY KEY clustered check stored somewhere
entry.content:
You should start a new thread for the SQL_NTS question. I have tested with the query from Wednesday (where you identified the issue as resolved - I note this only because of the multiple queries in thread).

Here are the bindings:

SQLBindCol(stmt, 1, SQL_C_WCHAR, 
    tableName, sizeof(tableName), &cbTable);
SQLBindCol(stmt, 2, SQL_C_ULONG, 
    &clusterIdx, sizeof(clusterIdx), &cbclustered);
SQLBindCol(stmt, 3, SQL_C_WCHAR, 
    idxName, sizeof(idxName), &cbIdxName);
SQLBindCol(stmt, 4, SQL_C_WCHAR, 
    idxType, sizeof(idxType), &cbIdxType);

  and the code that displays the results

printf( "%ls,%ls,%ls,ls%\n", 
    (wchar_t *)tableName,
    (cbclustered==SQL_NULL_DATA ? L"(NULL)":L"Clustered"),
    (wchar_t *)idxName,
    (wchar_t *)idxType );

And the actual output (note that I am testing a table name T with a primary key name T:

[C:\2026\forum\Clustered]odbcselect.exe
t,(NULL),t,ls

The second column reports (NULL) because the indicator for the clustered index column is returned as SQL_NULL_DATA.

entry.title: [Utilities] SAP Learning Curriculum for Utilities
entry.content:
The Utilities industry is evolving rapidly - and so is the way we learn in SAP for Utilities.

To better support our learners, we have introduced new and updated Utilities training courses, while older Utilities courses have been retired. This blog helps you understand what this change means for you and how to continue your learning journey with clarity and confidence.

Below is an overview of the key new Utilities courses, with links to both learning.sap.com (learning journeys & overviews) and training.sap.com (official SAP-delivered ILT / virtual classes):
 

1. S4IUT – Executing Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Utilities

  • End‑to‑end introduction to SAP S/4HANA Utilities business processes

    • Provides a comprehensive overview of core Utilities business processes in SAP S/4HANA
    • Covers Meter‑to‑Cash, Customer Engagement, Contract and Account Management, FI‑CA, and Device Management at process level
    • Focuses on how Utilities processes work end‑to‑end rather than on detailed configuration
    • Designed for:
      • Utilities consultants and business analysts
      • Project team members
      • Learners new to SAP S/4HANA Utilities

    Typically positioned as the primary entry point into SAP S/4HANA Utilities learning.

  • LSC Link: Discovering Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Utilities
  • ILT / Virtual training: https://training.sap.com/course/s4iut

2. IUT21 – Configuring Master Data and Basic Functions in SAP S/4HANA Utilities

  • Foundation course for Utilities configuration and core setup

    • Focuses on Utilities master data objects and basic configuration in SAP S/4HANA Utilities
    • Covers:
      • Business partners and contracts
      • Premises and connection objects
      • Basic process integration
    • Suitable for:
      • Implementation consultants
      • Configuration‑focused project team members
      • Learners moving from functional overview to system setup

    Typically positioned as the first configuration course following an end‑to‑end process overview.

  • LSC Link: Configuring Master Data and Basic Functions in SAP S/4HANA Utilit
  • ILT / Virtual training: https://training.sap.com/course/iut21

3. IUT22 – Configuring Device Management in SAP S/4HANA Utilities

  • In‑depth configuration of Utilities Device Management

    • Deep dive into Device Management in SAP S/4HANA Utilities
    • Covers:
      • Technical objects and installations
      • Meter reading, inspections, and technical processes
      • Integration of Device Management with commercial processes
    • Designed for:
      • Utilities technical consultants
      • Device Management specialists
      • Project roles focusing on metering and technical assets

    Typically positioned as an advanced follow‑up after core Utilities configuration.

  • LSC Link: Configuring Device Management in SAP S/4HANA Utilities
  • ILT / Virtual training: https://training.sap.com/course/iut22

4. IUT23 – Configuring Billing and Invoicing in SAP S/4HANA Utilities

  • Advanced course for Utilities billing and invoicing processes

    • Focuses on Utilities‑specific billing and invoicing in SAP S/4HANA
    • Covers:
      • Tariff structures and billing schemas
      • Consumption‑based billing
      • Integration between billing, invoicing, and FI components
    • Intended for:
      • Billing and invoicing consultants
      • Project team members responsible for revenue processes
      • Advanced Utilities configuration roles

    Typically positioned as an advanced configuration course, building on master data and device management knowledge.

  • LSC Link: Configuring Billing and Invoicing in SAP S/4HANA Utilities
  • ILT / Virtual training: https://training.sap.com/course/iut23 

S4IUD – Transitioning to SAP S/4HANA for Utilities

  • Understanding the transition from SAP ECC IS‑U to SAP S/4HANA Utilities

    • Designed for Utilities customers and partners planning or executing a system transition
    • Covers:
      • SAP S/4HANA Utilities architecture
      • Transition and conversion scenarios
      • Impact on business processes and system landscape
      • Introduction to Fiori and SAP BTP context for Utilities
    • Suitable for:
      • Architects and senior consultants
      • Project leads
      • Customers preparing for S/4HANA Utilities adoption

    Typically positioned as a strategic orientation course for Utilities transition projects.

  • LSC link: ​​Transitioning to SAP S/4HANA for Utilities​
  • ILT / Virtual training: https://training.sap.com/course/S4IUD
 

German market‑specific learning offering

IUT51 – SAP Market Communication for Utilities in the German Energy Market

  • Market‑specific introduction to SAP market communication processes for German utilities

    • Provides a structured introduction to SAP Market Communication (MaCo) in the deregulated German energy market, focusing on the regulatory and process context specific to Germany
    • Explains the evolution of market communication in SAP Utilities, including the role of energy market deregulation and its impact on Utilities business processes
    • Introduces the architecture and core components used by SAP to support market communication scenarios, including:
      • Application Process Engine (APE)
      • MaCo‑Cloud
    • Describes how regulatory changes have influenced SAP IS‑U and IDEX‑based processes, and how SAP is shifting these scenarios toward cloud‑based automation
    • Provides insight into key process flows and master data concepts used for market communication in Germany, based on SAP’s Utilities reference data model
    • Includes exposure to SAP tooling and content through:
      • SAP Fiori apps
      • Demonstrations of MSB billing scenarios
      • Exploration of SAP’s reference data structures for German Utilities market communication

    Designed for:

    • Architects
    • Consultants
    • Business users

    working with market communication processes in the German Utilities industry.

    This course is positioned as a specialized, market‑specific learning offering and builds on an existing understanding of SAP S/4HANA Utilities end‑to‑end business processes.

  • LSC link: Explore SAP Market Communication for Utilities in the German Energy Market

IUT580 – SAP Market Communication for Utilities, focus on Application Process Engine (APE)

  • Technical overview of SAP Market Communication for Utilities with focus on APE

    • Provides a comprehensive technical overview of SAP Market Communication for Utilities with a focus on the Application Process Engine (APE)
    • Explains the system architecture and technical fundamentals of SAP Market Communication solutions
    • Covers user interfaces, applications, and integration aspects used in market communication scenarios
    • Introduces APE architecture and functionality, including configuration, processes, checks, exceptions, and deadlines
    • Explains how to adapt standard market communication processes or create custom processes using APE, business objects, and Business Add‑Ins (BAdIs)

    Designed for:

    • Enterprise and solution architects
    • Senior technical and application consultants
  • Typically positioned as a technical deep‑dive course for designing, extending, and controlling SAP Market Communication processes using APE.
  • ILT / Virtual training: https://training.sap.com/course/iut580

What’s changing in Utilities learning?

Over the past years, SAP Utilities solutions, architectures, and business processes have significantly evolved. To reflect this:

  •  New Utilities courses and learning journeys are now available
  •  Older Utilities-specific courses have been retired and are no longer maintained
  •  The learning focus has shifted to modern SAP solutions, industry processes, and current best practices

This means that if you previously started learning Utilities with older courses, now is the right time to review and update your learning path.

Why new courses?

The new Utilities learning content is designed to:

  • Align with current SAP solution portfolios
  • Focus on end-to-end industry processes
  • Support learners in different roles (consultants, architects, business professionals)
  • Serve as a foundation for project readiness and continuous learning

Instead of isolated topics, the new courses help you understand:

  • How SAP addresses Utilities industry challenges today
  • Which topics are essential depending on your role
  • Where to continue learning next

What should you do if you learned with the old courses?

If you used older Utilities trainings in the past:

  • Consider them good historical context, but not a current learning baseline
  • Use the new courses to refresh and modernize your knowledge
  • Combine new Utilities learning with related SAP platform topics (for example analytics, integration, or cloud fundamentals)

This is a natural evolution and many learners are currently making the same transition.

You don’t have to do this alone

To support this transition, the Industries Learning Group was created as a dedicated place to talk about learning - not technical implementation.

In this group, you can:

  • Exchange with other Utilities learners
  • Ask questions like:
    • Which Utilities course should I start with now?
    • How do the new courses fit together?
    • What should I learn next after completing a course?
  • Share feedback on learning journeys and enablement content

The goal is simple: help each other navigate SAP learning for industries more easily.

How Utilities content is structured in the group

All posts related to Utilities are clearly marked:

  • Utilities in the title (for example: [Utilities] Learning Path Overview)
  • Utilities tags, so you can filter and follow relevant content

This makes it easy to stay focused on what matters to you.

Join the Industries Learning Group

If you are:

  • Starting your SAP Utilities learning journey
  • Transitioning from retired courses to the new ones
  • Looking for orientation before going deeper into technical topics
 👉 Join the Industries Learning Group
 👉 Follow Utilities‑related posts
 👉 Exchange with other learners

This group is your learning starting point.
For deep technical or implementation discussions, we will gladly guide you to the right expert communities.

Let’s learn SAP for Utilities - together.

Greg

entry.title: Purchase Card Integration with SAP Concur
entry.content:
Introduction: SAP Concur seamlessly integrates purchasing cards (P‑cards) with expense management by automatically importing and categorizing transactions. This automation simplifies expense review, reconciliation, and approval while enhancing visibility into spending trends for better data analysis and vendor negotiations. The system enforces policy rules, manages workflows, and aligns invoices with pre-approved purchases to ensure accuracy and compliance.

Follow the below steps to integrate Purchase Card with SAP Concur:

Step-1: Create the Purchase Card Policy

Navigate to Administration > Expense > Policies. Select any existing policy and click on “Copy Policy” to create new policy

Fill in all the required details to configure the policy. Review all the information entered and click on “Save” to create the Purchase card Policy.

Step 2: Configure Purchase Card Feed Import Definition

  • To integrate purchase card with SAP Concur, a Partner case must be raised with Concur for the feed setup.
  • Once the purchase card integration setup is completed, all purchase cards will be mapped automatically to the employees.

Step 3: Review Purchase Cards mapping to the Card Program

Ensure each purchase card account is correctly linked to its corresponding card program. Proper association enables accurate expense tracking, smoother reconciliation, and more reliable reporting.

Only users with the Company Card Administrator role have permission to assign individual purchasing card accounts to a program.

  1. Navigate to Administration > Company > Tools > Company Card > Manage Accounts.
  2. Search for purchase card accounts by using the Search in options such as Name on Card, Employee Name, or Employee ID as same as corporate cards

  1. Select the desired row and choose Modify to open the Modify Card Account dialog box.

  1. Select the appropriate Card Program Name (required).
  2. Click Save.

Step 4: Review Merchant Codes to Expense Types Mappings

Ensure all merchant category codes (MCCs) are correctly mapped to expense types per Purchase Card policy for accurate classification and compliance.

To review and update merchant code mappings:

  1. Go to Administration > Company > Tools > Company Card > Merchant Code Mapping.

  1. Verify all MCCs are present and correctly labelled, mapped to the right expense types.
  2. For each expense type, check its status:
  • If mapped, select it, click Modify to verify or add MCCs via Map New.
  • If Not Mapped, select it, click Modify > Map New, map respective merchant code.
  1. To configure MCC settings, select an expense type, click Modify, choose the MCC, and click Modify again.
  2. Review Expense Type & merchant code mappings and click “Next” to continue.

  1. Review the mappings, make any necessary adjustments, and then click Done.

Step 5: Validate Purchase Card Settings

Review the purchase card settings:

  1. Navigate to Administration > Company > Tools > Company Card.
  2. Select the Settings tab.

  1. Modify the settings accordingly.
  2. Click on “Save”

Benefits of Using Purchase Card Integration with SAP Concur

  1. Automatic Data Feed: P‑Card transactions are automatically imported from the bank into Concur, eliminating manual expense entry and reducing user errors.
  2. Real-Time Financial Insight: Finance teams get real‑time insight into purchasing activity across departments, vendors, and cost centers through dashboards and reports.
  3. Stronger Policy Compliance: Concur enforces expense rules (MCC restrictions, limits, receipt requirements) at transaction level, reducing non‑compliant spending.
  4. Reduced Invoice Processing Costs: P‑Cards minimize low‑value invoices and AP workload by shifting spend directly to card transactions.

General FAQs:

Q1. What is a Purchase Card (P-Card) in SAP Concur?

A:  A company-issued payment card for purchases, with transactions automatically imported into SAP Concur for employee review and processing.

Q2. Can employees delete P-Card transactions?

A:  No, transactions cannot be deleted but can be reconciled, flagged, commented on, or returned based on permissions.

Q3. What if an expense report is submitted without all P-Card transactions assigned?

A:  Concur may block submission or alert users; unassigned transactions remain in Available Expenses until reconciled.

Q4. How does SAP Concur ensure P-Card compliance?

A: By enforcing policies through rules, audits, receipt requirements, and approval workflows before reimbursement or reconciliation.

entry.title: Reconhecimento da receita na entrega de mercadoria
entry.content:
configurando o fluxo de faturamento antecipado estou feito (Entrega Futura) e o sistema está ignorando o diferencial da receita, registrando o crédito na conta de resultado logo na emissão da NF da Futura.

Cenário Atual (O Problema)

  • NF de Saída (Futura): O sistema está creditando diretamente a conta de Receita ( 3.01.01.01.01), quando deveria creditar a conta Redutora ( 1.01.03.01.03).

  • Entrega: Como a receita já foi confiável na nota, a entrega realiza apenas a baixa de estoque (Estoque vs CPV) e impostos, deixando de fazer o "swap" contábil (Redutora vs Receita).

O Objetivo (Fluxo Correto)

O objetivo é que a contabilização siga o princípio da competência, onde a receita só "nasce" com a saída física:

  1. Na NF de Saída (Futura):

    • Débito: PN (Cliente)

    • Crédito: 1.01.03.01.03 ((-) Clientes Nacionais Venda Futura) — Conta Redutora/Passivo.

  2. Na Entrega:

    • Débito: 1.01.03.01.03 (Limpa a conta redutora).

    • Crédito: 3.01.01.01.01 (Reconhece a Receita de Venda).

    • Mais os lançamentos de CPV e Impostos.

 

Ambiente:

  • Versão: SAP Business One versão 10.0 para SAP HANA (10.00.200) FP 2208.

entry.title: Re: Business Objects Enterprise 4.3 update from SP04 patc...

Hello guys
Today I repeated the upgrade but, unfortunately, it failed again, always with the same errors.
The upgrade, I remind you, is from BOE 4.3 SP04 patch 8 to BOE 4.3 SP05 patch 5.

The pre-activities I did in this last attempt are:
- All the checks indicated in the SAP note 1952120 have been carried out 
- Re-downloaded upgrade packages from the SAP portal
- Checked disk space
- Created a new local account, member of the local group "Administrators"

"D:\SAP BusinessObjects\tomcat\bin\tomcat9.exe"
"D:\SAP BusinessObjects\InstallData\setup.engine\plat64\ActionAgentProc.exe"
"D:\SAP BusinessObjects\InstallData\setup.engine\actionagentproc.exe"
"D:\SAP BusinessObjects\setup.exe"
"D:\SAP BusinessObjects\InstallData\setup.engine\setupengine.exe"

- I set the following registry key to 1 for the management of files with very long paths:
HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem\LongPathsEnabled
- I renamed the folder:
D:\SAP BusinessObjects\InstallData\InstallCache\ in InstallCache.bak 
to have the upgrade tool recreate it.
- I have disabled, on the D drive, the Windows Search Indexing.
- I removed the "read only" attribute from the D:\SAP BusinessObjects\InstallData folder.
- I started the Process Monitor tool.

In the dialog phase of the upgrade, immediately after entering the login credentials, I stopped the SIA, Tomcat and SQL Anywhere.

Again, after a few minutes, the "ErrorsAndWarnings.log" file was populated with the usual errors.
In the log file, I can see the following line:

Error: Could not move temp dest folder to dest folder.Error:   Temp dest folder: D:\SAP BusinessObjects\InstallData\tmp\webi.cdp.dsl_plugin-4.0-da-nuError:   Dest folder:    D:\SAP BusinessObjects\InstallData\InstallCache\webi.cdp.dsl_plugin-4.0-da-nu\14.3.5.5657\

And I can find a trace, in the Process Monitor logs with “ACCESS DENIED” result, about the same folder.

The problem is that I don't understand how to identify the cause of this ACCESS DENIED in the proc.mon. event. How can I do this? (Unfortunately this forum doesn't allow me to upload either CSV or PML files 😞 to show you).

Please help me.
Kind regards.
Andrea

entry.title: WM Public Cloud Storage Type Authorization & Stock Consolidation
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am working in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Warehouse Management.

I have two related requirements:

1) Authorization Requirement (Storage Type Level)

I need to control user access based on warehouse storage type.

Business requirement:

  • Users should only be able to:
    • View stock
    • Create and confirm warehouse tasks
  • Only for specific storage types, not the entire warehouse

My questions:

  • Is there any standard Public Cloud WM functionality to restrict user access at storage type level?
  • Can this be controlled using business roles, restriction fields, or IAM configuration?
  • If not, what is the recommended workaround?

2) Warehouse Bin Cleanup / Stock Consolidation / Rearrangement

Business requirement:

  • Consolidate partial quantities of the same product where possible
  • Reduce scattered stock across many bins
  • Free up unnecessary occupied bins
  • Move stock between bins to improve warehouse organization
  • Clean up bin utilization after normal inbound/outbound operations

In EWM, this requirement sounds close to rearrangement, consolidation, slotting/rearrangement, or warehouse optimization functionality. However, in Public Cloud WM, I cannot find a standard automatic process that analyzes current bin stock and proposes cleanup/consolidation warehouse tasks.

My questions:

  • Is there any standard Public Cloud WM functionality to automatically propose warehouse tasks for bin cleanup, consolidation, or rearrangement?
  • If not, what is the recommended workaround?

Any practical configuration approach or workaround would be appreciated.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: New Learning Journey: Implementing Joule Across Your Organizational System Landscape

We are excited to announce the new Learning Journey, "Implementing Joule Across Your Organizational System Landscape." This free, comprehensive program is tailored for consultants looking to lead SAP customers through the adoption and optimization of Joule, SAP’s AI solution for intelligent, connected workflows.

The journey includes step-by-step resources, from foundational knowledge of the Joule ecosystem to practical guidance on activating and customizing Joule in SAP SuccessFactors, SAP S/4HANA, and more. You will also learn how to enable advanced Joule features—such as document grounding and analytical insights—and extend its capabilities to non-SAP environments, all while maintaining robust governance and security.

As a consultant, you will gain expert-level, up-to-date knowledge, empowering you to deliver tangible business value to your clients.

 

Key Takeaways:

  • Understand Joule’s ecosystem, architecture, and integration across SAP and non-SAP systems.
  • Learn to activate, configure, and customize Joule for SAP SuccessFactors, SAP S/4HANA, SAP Ariba, SAP Concur, and SAP Signavio.
  • Enable advanced features like document grounding and analytics in the SAP landscape.
  • Troubleshoot common setup issues and stay current with Joule updates and resources.

entry.title: Destination Anywhere — A VS Code REST Client for SAP BTP Developers
entry.content:
Destination Anywhere

The REST client for SAP BTP developers.
Send HTTP and OData requests straight from VS Code — with zero-config destination resolution.

  • dest:// for BTP subaccount destinations
  • mdk:// for Mobile Services app destinations
  • Variables and environments
  • Sidebar browser
  • On-premise support

Why Destination Anywhere?

You're coding in VS Code, you need to test an API call against your BTP backend, and suddenly you're out of your editor — opening the BTP cockpit to look up a destination URL, switching to another tool to craft the request, grabbing a bearer token from somewhere, and forget about reaching on-premise systems that are only accessible from SAP BTP via Cloud Connector. Every round-trip breaks your flow.

Destination Anywhere keeps you where you already are. Type dest://MY_DESTINATION/path in a .dest file, press Cmd+Alt+R, and the extension resolves the real URL, handles authentication, and shows you the response — without ever leaving VS Code.

No cockpit tab. No manual tokens. No pasting URLs you looked up somewhere else. Just your editor.

Write a request, click Send, get the response. That's it.

From Install to First Request

Here's what happens the first time you open the extension — no setup guide needed, the sidebar walks you through it.

1. You're not logged in yet

Open the Destination Anywhere panel in the sidebar. If you're not logged in to Cloud Foundry, the extension tells you immediately and offers a one-click login button.

The sidebar detects your CF login state automatically.

2. Log in without leaving VS Code

Click Login with CF Tools and the CF sign-in form opens right inside VS Code. Enter your SSO passcode or credentials — no terminal needed.

CF Tools sign-in, right inside VS Code. SSO passcode or credentials — your choice.

3. No Destination Service? No problem.

Don't have a Destination Service instance? Most SAP BTP projects already have one — if yours does, the extension reuses it automatically and you'll never see this step. If not, here's why you need one: Destinations in SAP BTP are configured at the subaccount level in the BTP cockpit — they exist independently of any service instance. But the only way to read those destinations programmatically is through the Destination Service REST API. That API requires OAuth2 credentials (clientid, clientsecret, uri) which you can only obtain from a Destination Service instance and its service key in your CF space. So even though you never need an instance to create destinations, SAP BTP requires one just to query them. No instance, no credentials, no API access.

Full transparency — this extension and the router app are both open source. When you use on-premise destinations, Destination Anywhere deploys a small Express app to your CF space to relay requests through Cloud Connector. The entire source code — both the extension and the router app — is right here in this repository. The router is a single-file Express server (~100 lines) under router-app/ that you can read, audit, and even deploy manually yourself if you prefer.

Manual deployment is simple — go to the router-app folder, edit manifest.yml with your service instance names, then run cf push. See router-app/README.md for full instructions. Nothing hidden, nothing proprietary — you're always in control of what runs in your CF space.

When you open the sidebar, the extension scans your CF space for any existing destination service instance. If you already have one — from another project, a managed app router, or manual setup — it reuses that. Nothing is created, nothing changes.

If no instance exists, the sidebar shows a "Create Destination Service instance..." link. Click it and the extension creates a lite plan instance named dest-anywhere-dest with a service key dest-anywhere-dest-key. This is free, one-time, and persists across sessions.

No Destination Service instance in this space — click to create one on the free lite plan.

Auto-provisioning in action: the extension creates a service instance, generates keys, and loads your destinations.

4. Browse your destinations

After provisioning, your BTP destinations appear in the sidebar tree. Each destination shows its type (Internet / OnPremise) and authentication method. Right-click any destination to insert a sample request into your .dest file or view its full configuration.

Right-click a destination: insert a ready-to-send request or inspect its properties.

One click inserts a complete request block with the right URL and headers.

5. Inspect destination details

Want to see what a destination resolves to? Click Destination Info and a panel opens showing the resolved URL, authentication type, proxy settings, and all configuration properties — with sensitive values masked.

Full destination properties at a glance — URL, auth type, token endpoint, and more.

Mobile Services (mdk://)

Destination Anywhere also supports SAP Mobile Services destinations via the mdk:// protocol. The first time you use it, the extension opens your browser for SAP BTP authentication — no extra confirmation needed.

Your browser opens to the SAP BTP login page. Once authenticated, the sidebar shows your Mobile Services apps and their destinations alongside BTP destinations. Use mdk://AppId/DestName/path to send requests:

GET mdk://SAM2405/DEST_SAM2405_PPROP/MyEquipments?$top=10&$format=json
Accept: application/json

Mobile Services authenticated. Both dest:// and mdk:// requests in a single .dest file.

Usage Examples

BTP Destinations (dest://)

@SYSTEM = MY_S4_SYSTEM
@service = /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER

### Metadata
GET dest://{{system}}{{service}}/$metadata
Accept: application/xml

### Business partners with filter + expand
GET dest://{{system}}{{service}}/A_BusinessPartner?$filter=BusinessPartnerCategory eq '1'&$expand=to_BusinessPartnerAddress&$top=5&$format=json
Accept: application/json

### Create a business partner
POST dest://{{system}}{{service}}/A_BusinessPartner
Content-Type: application/json

{"BusinessPartnerCategory": "1", "BusinessPartnerFullName": "Acme Corp"}

Mobile Services Destinations (mdk://)

### Equipment list via MDK
GET mdk://SAM2405.SAM.WIN/DEST_SAM2405_PPROP/MyEquipments?$top=10&$format=json
Accept: application/json

### Create a notification
POST mdk://SAM2405.SAM.WIN/DEST_SAM2405_PPROP/Notifications
Content-Type: application/json

{"NotificationType": "PM", "Description": "Equipment failure reported via MDK"}

Plain HTTP (no BTP)

@Host = https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com

### Get a post
GET {{host}}/posts/1

### Create a post
POST {{host}}/posts
Content-Type: application/json

{"title": "Hello from Destination Anywhere", "body": "Works with any HTTP endpoint.", "userId": 1}

Variables and Environments

Define @Var = value inline, use .env files, or configure per-environment variable sets in VS Code settings:

"destinationAnywhere.environmentVariables":
  "dev": { "system": "MY_DEV_SYSTEM", "base": "/sap/opu/odata/sap" }
  "prod": { "system": "MY_PROD_SYSTEM", "base": "/sap/opu/odata/sap" }

Then in your .dest file:

### Switches based on active environment
GET dest://{{system}}{{base}}/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BusinessPartner?$top=5

Switch via Command Palette → Destination Anywhere: Switch Environment.

On-Premise Destinations

On-premise destinations route through SAP Cloud Connector, which is only reachable from inside Cloud Foundry. Destination Anywhere gives you two options:

Router app (recommended): One-click deploy of a lightweight Express app to your CF space. It uses SAP Cloud SDK to tunnel requests through Cloud Connector. XSUAA authentication is enforced. Use this when you have no local Cloud Connector or VPN. View the source code.

Direct mode: Sends requests directly to the destination URL using resolved credentials. Use this when a local Cloud Connector or VPN can reach the backend.

Deploy the router from the sidebar: expand BTP Destinations, and click Deploy On-Premise Router. The extension provisions Connectivity and XSUAA service instances automatically.

Key Features

  • dest:// protocol — Route requests through SAP BTP subaccount destinations. The extension resolves the real URL and injects authentication headers (Basic, OAuth2, SAML, Principal Propagation) automatically.
  • mdk:// protocol — Send requests through SAP Mobile Services App Router. Authenticate once via browser, then fire OData requests against MDK app destinations.
  • Sidebar panel — Browse all BTP destinations and Mobile Services apps in a tree view. Right-click to insert a sample request or view destination details.
  • On-premise routing — Access on-premise systems behind Cloud Connector. Deploy a lightweight router app to your CF space with one click — or use direct mode with a local Cloud Connector / VPN.
  • Variables and environments — Define @Var = value in your file, use .env files, or configure per-environment variable sets in settings. Switch environments from the Command Palette.
  • Syntax highlighting — Dedicated .dest language with a TextMate grammar — methods, URLs, dest:// / mdk:// schemes, headers, variables, and JSON bodies are all highlighted.
  • CodeLens — Click "Send Request" above any request block. Or press Cmd+Alt+R.
  • Smart autocomplete — HTTP methods, common headers, content types, and dest:// / mdk:// URL snippets.
  • Response panel — Status code (color-coded), response time, size, headers table, and pretty-printed body with JSON syntax highlighting and XML indentation. Copy body to clipboard in one click.
  • CF guard — Detects CF CLI installation and login state before making any BTP calls. Offers one-click login via CF Tools or a terminal fallback. Log out from the sidebar title bar.
  • Auto-provisioning — No Destination Service instance? The extension creates one for you (lite plan). Service keys are created and rotated as needed.
  • Caching — Resolved destinations, service credentials, and OAuth tokens are cached with configurable TTL. Clear everything with one command.

Commands

All commands are available in the Command Palette (Cmd+Shift+P / Ctrl+Shift+P😞

  • Destination Anywhere: Send Request — Send the request under the cursor
  • Destination Anywhere: Switch Environment — Change the active variable environment
  • Destination Anywhere: Clear Destination Cache — Purge all cached destinations, credentials, and tokens
  • Destination Anywhere: Login to Cloud Foundry — Open CF login via CF Tools or terminal
  • Destination Anywhere: Logout from Cloud Foundry — Log out from CF and clear all cached credentials
  • Destination Anywhere: Login to Mobile Services — Authenticate with SAP Mobile Services via browser
  • Destination Anywhere: Open Destinations Panel — Focus the sidebar tree view
  • Destination Anywhere: Create Destination Service instance — Create a Destination Service lite instance in the current CF space
  • Destination Anywhere: Deploy On-Premise Router — Deploy the Express-based router app for on-premise connectivity
  • Destination Anywhere: Uninstall On-Premise Router — Remove the router app and its service instances

Keyboard shortcut: Cmd+Alt+R / Ctrl+Alt+R → Send Request

Configuration

  • destinationAnywhere.timeout (default: 30000) — Request timeout in milliseconds
  • destinationAnywhere.followRedirects (default: true) — Follow HTTP redirects
  • destinationAnywhere.rejectUnauthorized (default: true) — Reject unauthorized SSL certificates
  • destinationAnywhere.defaultHeaders (default: {}) — Headers included in every request
  • destinationAnywhere.environmentVariables (default: {}) — Per-environment variable sets
  • destinationAnywhere.activeEnvironment (default: "") — Currently active environment
  • destinationAnywhere.destinationServiceUrl (default: "") — Manual Destination Service URL override (auto-discovered by default)
  • destinationAnywhere.mobileServicesUrl (default: "") — Manual Mobile Services URL override
  • destinationAnywhere.destinationCacheTTL (default: 300) — Destination cache TTL in seconds
  • destinationAnywhere.logLevel (default: "info") — Log level: off, error, warn, info, debug

Requirements

  • VS Code 1.87+
  • CF CLI — for dest:// and mdk:// features (install guide)
  • SAP BTP subaccount

Plain HTTP requests (https://...) work without CF CLI or any SAP services.

Installation

Install from the Visual Studio Code Marketplace:

  1. Open VS Code
  2. Press Ctrl+Shift+X (Windows/Linux) or Cmd+Shift+X (macOS) to open the Extensions view
  3. Search for Destination Anywhere
  4. Click Install

Or install from the command line: code --install-extension aydin-ozcan.destination-anywhere

VS Code Marketplace | GitHub Repository | Apache 2.0 License

entry.title: Re: SAP AFC - Open/Close periods with RFOB5200 in SAP Pub...
entry.content:
Dear @Sven_Lausen,

RFOB5200 is known from on-premise / private cloud scenarios. In Public Cloud, SAP confirms that the application job template SAP_FAC_GL_MAINTPERIOD / SAP Job for Posting Period Maintenance exists only for technical reasons and is not meant for direct use. SAP also states that neither direct API scheduling nor scheduling via Schedule General Ledger Jobs gives the expected result

The supported Public Cloud app is Manage Posting Periods F2293 which SAP explicitly names as the app to open, close and schedule posting periods

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3371464

For special periods, SAP also confirms a limitation in Manage Posting Periods F2293 when the item contains special periods, recurrence scheduling can fail and the periods must be maintained manually or scheduled one by one

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2896867

The practical Public Cloud approach is therefore:

  1. Use Manage Posting Periods F2293 to open/close FI posting periods.
  2. If scheduling is required, use the scheduling functionality available from Manage Posting Periods F2293, not RFOB5200
  3. In SAP AFC, model this as a manual closing activity, for example a task instructing the user to execute or verify the activity in Manage Posting Periods F2293. The RFOB5200-based job task is not the supported Public Cloud approach for this process

Summary:

In Public Cloud the missing RFOB5200 job in AFC is expected. The SAP-supported approach is to maintain and schedule posting periods through Manage Posting Periods F2293 AFC can orchestrate the activity as a closing task but the actual Public Cloud period maintenance should not be based on RFOB5200

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Does PRIMARY KEY clustered check stored somewhere
entry.content: SQLUINTEGER since the underlying column is an unsigned integer , and the TargetType SQL_C_ULONG is also unsigned integer. I am not displaying the value return for that column but I am using the SQL_NULL_DATA to report either 'NULL' or 'CLUSTERED' as it is sufficient proof that the correct StrLen_or_IndPtr is returned for NULL.

entry.title: Re: To get Services for Object icon for VD51/VD52.
entry.content:
Hello,
The GOS is not supported in transactions VD51/VD52 indeed. That is a missing functionality.
If you would consider creating some custom transaction in order to reach your requirement, the GOS is handled by CL_GOS_MANAGER. Below is a call stack of when the constructor of this class is called from VA01:
 
6 METHOD CONSTRUCTOR CL_GOS_MANAGER================CP CL_GOS_MANAGER================CM001 10
5 METHOD GOS_PUBLISH_OBJECT CL_SD_SLS_GOS=================CP CL_SD_SLS_GOS=================CM00C 31
4 METHOD GOS_START_DOCUMENT CL_SD_SLS_GOS=================CP CL_SD_SLS_GOS=================CM00E 18
3 MODULE (PAI) GOS_DISPLAY SAPMV45A MV45A_GOS_DISPLAYI01 11
2 PAI SCREEN 0101 SAPMV45A 86
1 TRANSACTION VA01(VA01) 0
 
In the constructor, if FM SUSR_USER_LOGONDATA_GET returns SY-SUBRC = 0, the GOS button will then be displayed.
 
Best regards.

entry.title: SAP Analytics - Data Mesh
entry.content:
Hello friends,

Do you know what is Data Mesh and the new possibilities with SAP Analytics? Bellow, following one basic description of SAP Intelligent Data Mesh and links of our SAP Community with more amazing detail of Data Mesh with SAP Data Warehouse Cloud, SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Master Data Governance, SAP Data Intelligence Cloud, SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere.

Good read and enjoy your journey with SAP Analytics.

SAP Intelligent Data Mesh

SAP Intelligent Data Mesh is SAP's approach to implementing data mesh architecture within enterprise environments. It combines modern data management practices with SAP's existing ecosystem. Here's what you need to know:

Key Concepts

Data Mesh Principles Applied:

  • Decentralized data ownership - Business domains own their data rather than centralized IT
  • Domain-driven design - Data organized around business capabilities
  • Data as a product - Treats data with the same rigor as software products
  • Self-service analytics - Enables business users to access and use data independently

Core Components

  1. Data Fabric - Unified integration layer connecting disparate data sources
  2. Metadata Management - Catalogs and governs data across domains
  3. Governance Framework - Ensures quality, security, and compliance
  4. Analytics & AI Integration - Embedded machine learning and analytics capabilities
  5. Self-Service Tools - Low-code/no-code interfaces for domain teams

Key Benefits

✓ Faster time-to-insight ✓ Improved data quality through domain ownership ✓ Reduced dependency on central IT teams ✓ Better scalability ✓ Enhanced collaboration across business units

SAP Tools Involved

  • SAP Analytics Cloud
  • SAP Data Warehouse Cloud
  • SAP Master Data Governance
  • SAP Datasphere (unified data experience platform)

Use Case

Particularly valuable for large enterprises with multiple business units needing agile, decentralized data access while maintaining governance.

Links

More than just a hype: Data Mesh as a new approach to increase agility in value creation from data

Data Mesh with SAP Data Warehouse Cloud

Data Mesh with SAP HANA Cloud

Data Mesh with SAP Master Data Governance

Data Mesh with SAP Data Intelligence Cloud

Data Mesh with SAP Analytics Cloud

Data Mesh with SAP Datasphere

entry.title: SAP CPQ - Regular Maintenance - Announcement - 02 May 2026
entry.content:
Hi Team,

this is not very user friendly; I just want to post a query on CPQ and there's no clear indication where to post it. Please fix that. 

 

Great Lakes Cheese 

CRM ID: 170665
ERP ID: 303388
 
Customer reviewed changes coming up with the CPQ upgrade in May.  The one below caught their attention as something GLC CPQ users would likely benefit from.  Looking at the release notes, it says they have to submit a request to SAP Support to enable it. 

Change:

Parallel Quoting
Users can work on multiple quotes simultaneously across separate browser tabs. This eliminates the need to choose between abandoning current work or losing context when referencing other quotes.

 
Questions:
1. Is there a way for to get more information about this functionality or a preview before they request it is activated?

2. If there's no details about the new Parallel Quoting functionality and if they request it to be activated in the Test tenant and find it causes issues, can it subsequently be deactivated?  

 Can someone please advise? 

Thanks and regards,
Marc

entry.title: SAP BDC: Share table from disk storage to HANA lake file spaces
entry.content:

Can someone guide me step by step process and are there any limitation to request.

entry.title: A recap of my amazing 4 stops in Brazil for the 3rd annual LATAM Roadshow 2026
entry.content:
As you may have seen we had an amazing 3rd SAP CodeJam LATAM roadshow organized for the third year by @ajmaradiaga and while I couldn't be there in March I made it down for not only some CodeJams but also an SAP Inside Track.  I got to meet with some amazing SAP Community Members, a few of our SAP Champions and some of my Community Relations colleagues.  Here is my stop by stop recap.  

Day 1 in #Brazil of my third annual LATAM roadshow for is completed (not counting travel days 😜 ) where I got to see some of the amazing sites in #Curitiba before heading to a meetup with a few colleagues, organizers, and an amazing sponsor: CHPro


The AI Core CodeJam with @thomas_jung and @RichHeilman  was sponsored by the amazing team at CHPro Solutions led by Calixto Herkert and our location host university Escola Politécnica PUCPR.

The pictures can’t capture the amazing energy and love from all the participants and their enjoyment going though the #CodeJam !
Many thanks to the volunteer photographer (I included some of her pics, abrigado!)

The organizers of the SAP Community Curitiba deserve all the credit, from @SimoneAzevedo   to @LuizSouza_cwb   and @AdrianoCWB .  Such a well organized event. I can’t wait to be back in Curitiba but now I’m off to #Campinas for an SAP Inside Track. SAP Community  is strong!

____

Stop number two of the LATAM roadshow for me was an amazing trip to a Campinas, a city just north to the north of São Paulo. The event is an SAP inside track (SAP Inside Track Campinas Inside Track Brasil 🇧🇷 just outside of Sao Paulo ) and I get to connect with my colleagues @antonia_zorn @noravonthenen and @Vitaliy-R  , as well as continue with @RichHeilman and @thomas_jung , but unfortunately for Rich and Thimas, this will be their last stop of the tour.

 

Also exciting is getting to meet so many community members, especially our SAP champions @RobertaFernande  , @MRobalinho , and @Marssel700 . Truly, there were so many volunteers and an amazing campus that hosted our event lots of great sponsors and familiar faces form the last two years. Kicking off today with a co-presentation with Antonia was fun and then watching my colleagues present, as well as so many other customers and partners from the SAP community. It was so many people learning and sharing about SAP  in the Brazil community, it’s just something that I cannot express enough, how vibrant the Volunteer SAP Community  is and the interest of the attendees. The food was amazing. The capybara‘s were amazing and I appreciated every conversation and photo, but I’m sure you could tell that….


Next, we got together for a meet up at an amazing local brewery. I don’t even think there was a way to count the number of people who came over and enjoyed time together socializing.


But like all good things this stop had to come to an end so me, Nora, Vitaliy and Antonia packed up and are off to our next stop Porto Alegre. See you there!
_____

My 3rd stop of the LATAM CodeJam tour was continuing with Vitaliy Rudnytskiy  , Nora von Thenen  and Antonia Zorn  to São Leopoldo/ Porto Alegre. This southern Brazil city and its people are amazing but wow the SAP Labs office is sooooo amazing.
 

 


We had two CodeJams and a meetup between Monday/Tuesday. A packed house both days. And a special shoutout to our local support who’ve been with me in Curitiba and will also join at my last sip of Recife. Vitor Ugo Fiori  Vitória Cristofolli.

 

 

 

 
The CodeJams were amazing and the attendees so great at participating and engaging both days. It was also great to attend a meetup at a local German themed fun event center with billiards and bowling. And I got to eat my first Xis thanks to Vitor’s guidance. I also got to trip in dramatic fashion in Parque Moinhos de Vento while out for a jog with Vitaliy, and thank goodness he was right there to pick me up as any good Developer Advocate would do!

 

 


On a side note, I also was lucky enough to meet some local SAP colleagues who before now I’ve only seen online. Thank you for your time and I wish we had more chances to meet as we are all SAP family.

 

p.s. When Antonia says “Did you see the colleague in the next room with blue hair?” Well I think you know what happened next, right Ângela Schmidt

__

And stop 4 for me of the #LATAM  #Brazil roadshow is in the books. It’s actually the end of the whole tour for the team in Brazil which is sad but was such a great time for the SAP Community . A special shoutout to Vitória Cristofolli  and Vitor Ugo Fiori  for their support.

Arriving from Porto Alegre to #Recife was such a dramatic change. A northern coastal town with such a different look and feel to the architecture and of course the ocean views

I did a quick tour of the downtown and actually got to see the first synagogue ever built in the Americas (Synagoga Kahal Zur Israel) which showed me so much personal history I never thought I’d experience and will share with my Rabbi.



Then it was back to work. Me, Nora von Thenen  , Vitaliy Rudnytskiy  and Antonia Zorn  were hosted by the amazing team at Accenture led by João Otto Gethmann Jr  .


The attendees were so warm and attentive and enjoyed learning from Witalij on day 1 and Nora on day 2. Me and Antonia shared about the community and also had the pleasure of seeing so many locals at our meetup on a rooftop.

Stories were shared, local food and drink consumed and code discussions all around.  SAP Community, #barbaAzul shall return…

entry.title: Re: 3XK In-House Repair – Spare Parts Consumption Timing...
entry.content:
Good day @Wei_Wum8 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, what you explained is largely expected for 3XK.

In the standard 3XK process, stock service parts are planned on the repair order. When the repair order is released, the system supports availability check and reservation for stock service parts. The actual goods movement for the consumed spare part is posted through the repair confirmation. In general repair confirmations are posted against repair order line items and that spares costs are posted automatically. Also, time sheet entries, goods issues, and expenses can be posted from the confirmation onto the repair order. So, this basically means that:

  • The spare part can be planned and reserved before the repair is executed.
  • The physical warehouse issue can happen operationally before the technician performs the repair.
  • The system posting of consumption is normally triggered by the repair confirmation.
  • The repair confirmation is the business step that records the actual consumed spare parts.
  • A separate standard “warehouse receipt by technician” or “parts handover confirmation” step is not provided in the lean 3XK flow.

 

So, to answer your specific questions:

Question 1: Is this the expected standard behaviour for 3XK?

Answer: As mentioned above, yes, it is. In the standard lean 3XK flow, spare parts are consumed through the repair confirmation. The process is designed around planning the repair, releasing the repair order, performing the repair, and confirming the actual resource consumed. That means, the service confirmation is therefore the key step where the spare part consumption becomes financially and logistically relevant.

Question 2: Is there any standard way to separate goods issue from service confirmation or add a receipt/confirmation step?

Answer: In the standard 3XK process, there is no separate standard step to post the goods issue independently from the repair confirmation from the same stock service part item. There is also not dedicated standard app step to confirm that the service team received the spare part before it is consumed. If your business needs this control, the practical options are for you to:

  • Use the reservation and availability check to support planning.
  • Use operational warehouse handling outside the repair confirmation step, for example physical picking or internal process control.
  • Use custom fields or custom logic to track a warehouse handover or availability status in the in-house repair process. KBA 3482680 confirms that there is currently no dedicated app to track the availability status of in-house repairs, but you can extend the Manage In-House Repairs app with a custom field and BAdI CRMS4_REPA_CUST_I_MODIFY.
  • If you require a more formal logistic execution separation is required, this will usually need to be evaluated as a fit to standard or as an extensibility requirement rather than a standard 3XK goods issue step.

Question 3: Does the reservation provide any priority or protection for service orders?

Answer: The reservation helps the system reflect a requirement from the stock service part. The system offers availability check and reservation functionality for stock service parts in the In-House repair process. However, do not take this as a full warehouse execution or physical stock handover confirmation. It supports availability planning and reservation of the quantity you required, but it does not replace picking, staging, or a separate confirmation that the technician has physically received the part.

Explaining the Process Steps for In-House Repairs

Whether other demands such as sales order can still consume the stock will depend on the ATP and its related availability check behaviour, the material setup, and the sequence of demands. Therefore, if your business requirement is strictly to protect your stock, you should test this specifically with a competing sales order or outbound demand in the same plant and storage location.

Here are references for more guidance

3580897 - Material Reservation Not Created from Service Orders - SAP for Me

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-in-house-service-processes-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-service/explaining-additional-in-house-repair-functionality_ef11bc79-5f07-4f82-901e-7ee6ae805ec3

 

here is a quick guide:

open Manage In-House Repairs, create or open an in-house repair, add the repair object and confirm the in-house repair so that the repair object moves the next process step.

In Perform Prechecks, select the repair object, choose the follow-up decision to start repair and create the repair order.

Open Plan Repairs, search for the repair order, add a stock service part item, check the quantity, plant and item category and release the repair order. Check whether the reservation is created after you have released it.

Either in Display Stock, Monitor Material Coverage or Stock Overview app you have in your tenant. Search for the spare part material, check unrestricted stock before creatin the reservation, check the stock and requirement after the repair order is released.

Finally, open Perform Repairs again, select the repair order you assigned to the technician, choose Create Service Confirmation, enter the actual consumed spare part quantity and post the confirmation.

After you have posted the service confirmation:

  • compare the stock before confirmation,
  • reservation after repair order release,
  • stock after confirmation,
  • repair order status and,
  • material document or goods movement reference.

This will show that the system reserves or plans the spare part earlier but posts the actual goods movement when the repair confirmation is posted. The next step I would recommend is for you to test the same material with a competing sales order or other demand after the repair reservation is created. This will show whether the current ATP and reservation setup gives enough practical protection for your warehouse process. If your requirement Is a mandatory warehouse issue and technician receipt before repair, then the standard 3XK process will likely need an extensibility or process workaround rather than a configuration switch.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: WM Public Cloud Storage Type Authorization & Stock Co...
entry.content:
Hello Adel-Rihan,

I can give you some input regarding 1) Authorization Requirement (Storage Type Level).

 

My answers to your specific questions:

Is there any standard Public Cloud WM functionality to restrict user access at storage type level? No, “Storage Type” does not exist as a restriction.

Can this be controlled using business roles, restriction fields, or IAM configuration? You may set up business roles and their restriction fields based on a combination of different storage parameters like Plant, Storage Location, Shipping Point / Receiving Point, Warehouse Number/Warehouse Complex and Movement Types.

If not, what is the recommended workaround? The recommended workaround is restriction definition based on the above-mentioned parameters like Storage Location. Additionally, you may work with Movement Types as restrictions to define more precisely which processes can be performed.

 

You may use the Apps "IAM Information System" or "Display Restriction Types" to search for available restrictions. 

By using the available restrictions you can create an authorization concept according to your specific needs.

However, not every app / process checks restrictions in the exact same way. So after defining your business roles, it is necessary to run some checks to verify if your requirement is fulfilled.

 

Related resources:

  • Display Restriction Types | SAP Help Portal
  • The Movement Type Concept | SAP Help Portal

 

I hope this helps.

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: Re: How to add new variants in the apps Manage Archiving...
entry.content:
Hello @li_zhang8ed3ad 

Thank you for your question.

In summary, the Add New Variant button is normally only available when the user has the correct authorization and when you can select an archiving object in the app.

Consult these references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/73d60bd1893d489cb896bcec22a50ea5.html?locale=en-US

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/performing-advanced-configuration-and-role-management-in-sap-s4hana-cloud-public-edition-ehs-environment-management/developing-business-roles-using-templates

From your first screenshot, the left panel shows No matches found under Archiving Objects. This is important. If no archiving object is available to the user, the app has nothing to create a variant for, so the Add New Variant button will not appear. So, this basically means:

  • The issue is most likely to authorisation or scope.
  • The app itself may be visible but you (the user) may not have sufficient authorization to see or maintain archiving objects.
  • SAP_BR_DATA_PRIVACY_SPECIALIST is a business role template, not always an already-created business role in the customer tenant.
  • If you did not create a business role from this template, searching for it in Maintain Business Roles may not return no result.
  • You should manually create a customer business role from the delivered template or add the required ILM and data privacy business catalogs

Here are references for more guidance

3340679 - Changing the Batch Management Indicator of a material in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

2986171 - Error message R111022 in the app Block Master Data - Business Partner - SAP for Me

2702011 - Deleting Employee and Contingent Worker Data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

To answer your questions:

Question 1: But in our system, the button [Add New Variants] is not showed.

Answer: As mentioned above the likely reason is that your user does not have the required business role, business catalog, or write authorization for archiving variant maintenance.

Question 2: Why the related business role [SAP_BR_DATA_PRIVACY_SPECIALIST] does not exist in our system, too?

Answer: The reason is the business role is a delivered role template, not necessarily an active busines role already created in your tenant. The common approach is to open Maintain Business Roles, choose Create from Template, select the relevant delivered template, and create your own customer business role from it. Only use templates mainly as a starting point and then tailor the productive role to your company’s authorization concept.

I suggest you check the following:

  • Whether the business role was created from template SAP_BR_DATA_PRIVACY_SPECIALIST.
  • Whether your user is assigned to that created business role.
  • Whether the business role contains the relevant ILM, archiving, and data privacy business catalogs.
  • Whether restrictions are maintained with sufficient write access
  • Whether the required scope item or archiving functionality is active in your tenant.
  • Whether the app library entry you checked is for the correct product version and release.

 Here is a quick guide:

In Maintain Business Roles, do not search for SAP_BR_DATA_PRIVACY_SPECIALIST,

choose from the Template, search for template.

Create a new customer role, for example Z_DATA_PRIVACY_SPECIALITS. Then save the role.

Go to Assigned Business Users, add your user, go to Assigned Business Catalogs and verify that the data privacy and ILM related catalogs are included. In Maintain Restrictions, check that read , write, and value help access are not restricted in a way that prevents maintaining archiving variant and save the role.

Log off and log on again after you have assigned the role. Open Managing Archiving Variants and check whether archiving objects are now displayed in the left panel. Select the archiving object and check whether the Add New Variant button appears.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Single billing document to be created with TAN & TAD...
entry.content:
Dear Sravan,

In this scenario, the requirement cannot be achieved through standard configuration alone without using a combination approach.

Business Scenario:

  • Sales Order contains two line items:
    • Item 10 → TAN (delivery-relevant)
    • Item 20 → TAD (not delivery-relevant)
  • During delivery creation, only Item 10 is normally processed.
  • However, the requirement is to include both items (TAN + TAD) in a single billing document created from delivery.

Standard Behavior:
In standard SAP, delivery-related billing (VTFL) only copies delivery-relevant items. Therefore, TAD (text item) will not be copied into billing via standard copy control.

Proposed Solution:

To meet the requirement, a custom item category approach is used:

  1. Create a Custom Item Category (e.g., ZTAD):
    • Configure in VOV7 with appropriate billing relevance (delivery-based).
    • Assign item category group LEIS to treat it as a service-type item.
  2. Item Category Determination (VOV4):
    Maintain combinations such as:
    • Sales Doc Type + NORM → Default: TAN, Manual: ZTAD
    • Sales Doc Type + LEIS → Default: ZTAD
  3. Delivery Item Category Configuration (0VLP):
    For ZTAD, deactivate:
    • Relevant for picking
    • Storage location required
    • Storage location determination

This ensures the item appears in delivery but does not participate in logistics execution.

  1. Assign Delivery Item Category (0184):
    Ensure ZTAD is properly mapped for delivery processing.

Copy Control Settings:

VTLA (Sales Order → Delivery):

  • Order Requirement: 001
  • Combination Requirement: 051
  • Header Data: 001

For both TAN and ZTAD at item level:

  • Order Requirement: 101
  • Item Data: 101
  • Business Data: 02
  • Update Document Flow: Active
  • Pos./Neg. Qty: +

VTFL (Delivery → Billing):

  • Copying Requirement (Header): 003
  • Copy Item Number: Active

For both TAN and ZTAD:

  • Copying Requirement: 004
  • Billing Quantity: G (Delivery quantity)
  • Pos./Neg. Qty: +
  • Pricing Type: G (Copy pricing from reference)

Expected Outcome:

  • TAN item is processed normally with inventory and COGS impact.
  • ZTAD item flows into delivery as a non-logistical item.
  • During billing, both TAN and ZTAD are picked from delivery into a single invoice, with ZTAD contributing only to pricing and revenue without inventory impact.

Regards,

Soumya Nath

entry.title: Re: How to allocate Capacity Units (CU) to SAC user licenses
entry.content:
Dear hiromi_nakamura79c62f,

Hope you are well. Thank you for your message.

To address this scenario effectively, it is recommended reviewing the following points to ensure a resolution:

1. If this SAC system is rewired to BDC as per Updating Your SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud Subscriptions to SAP Business Data Cloud | SAP Help Portal or directly created in BDC as per Provisioning SAP Analytics Cloud | SAP Help Portal,  it is recommended to first check blog How to use SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity Unit E... - SAP Community and How to use SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity Unit E... - SAP Community to have a more comprehensive understanding on how to estimate the capacity units for different SAC license types and how to distribute capacity unit across different applications. After the estimation, it is required to allocate capacity units for SAC in SAP For Me as per help document Editing Your Provisioned SAP Analytics Cloud Application.

2. If your SAC system operates as a Subscription-Based SAP-Managed System and has not been rewired or created in BDC, it is necessary to address any related license allocations or purchases from the perspective of your SAC contract. We recommend reaching out to the Account Owner or Quote Owner associated with your SAC contract to review and discuss any necessary license purchasing or allocation requirements. This approach ensures all necessary licensing aspects are appropriately managed. 

Thank you for your time.

Best wishes,

Lena

entry.title: ACDOCA_M_EXTRACT vs MBEW
entry.content:
Can we use the ACDOCA_M_EXTRACT to get the price of a product for a stock report.
We are using materials with
batch specific unit of measure (FERT),
with batch valuation (FERT),
without batch valuation (HALB, ROH),
With batch (FERT),
without batch (ROH),
project stock,
normal stock,
sales order stock.
Is it good to take the perunit price of a product from this report using the current period (999.9999 - FISCYEARPER). Or if its MBEW then how to consider all the stock table and scenarios to pick the stock

  • MARD: Storage Location Data for Material (current stock, e.g., LABST).
  • MCHB: Batch Stocks (Batch-managed materials).
  • MSPR/MSSQ: Project Stock / Total Project Stock
  • MSKA/MSSA: Sales Order Stock / Total Sales Order Stock
  • MSLB/MSSL: Special Stocks with Vendor / Total Special Stocks with Vendor
  • MSKU: Special Stocks with Customer (Consignment/Returnable Package)
  • MKOL: Special Stocks from Vendor (Consignment)
  • MSCA: Sales Orders on Hand with Vendor 
  • MSTB: Stock in Transit
  • MSTE: Stock in Transit Sales Order
  • MSTQ: Stock in Transit Project
Is it need to fetch values from all the belwo Valuation Tables ?
  • EBEW: Sales Order Stock Valuation
  • QBEW: Project Stock Valuation
  • OBEW: Valuated Stock with Subcontractor
  • MBEW: Material Valuation (Standard)

entry.title: Re: Problem reinstalling SAP ASE
entry.content:
@oneeyeman1

The trial license (aka EValutation license) can be updated/extended in 1-year intervals @ https://go.support.sap.com/minisap/#/minisap.  I have a pair of ASE installations that are a few years old and they run just fine on my (annually updated) EV license.

As for the express (XE) license, the license file has a generic key that should work on any host (ie, HOSTID=ANY).  In the past I've been able to copy the express license file (*XE.lic) to newer ASE installations and ASE successfully started up on said license (ie, the newer ASE software still recognized the XE license). [NOTE: It's been a couple years since I tested this workaround so I don't know if it still works with the latest ASE distributions.]
